HOOK · Particle Motion (3 States)
粒子运动（三态）
HOOK
•
Real-world anchor — a smell reaching you from across a room.
真实引子——味道从房间另一头飘到你这。
•
When food is cooking on one side of a room, the smell travels to the other side on its own.
一边在煮东西,味道会自己跑到另一边。
•
Why?
为什么?带着味道的气体粒子在动——又快又乱地到处跑。
•
The gas particles carrying the smell are moving — fast and in all directions.
GLOBAL smell spreading through air (global commons).opening story / flesh → B1b.
LEARN · Particle Motion (3 States)
粒子运动（三态）
LEARN
•
Last lesson (L08) we saw how particles are ARRANGED.
上一课(L08)学了粒子怎么"排"。
•
Arrangement is about where particles sit.
排列说的是"站在哪"。
•
This lesson is about whether and how they MOVE.
这一课说的是"动不动、怎么动"。
•
S
固体——粒子只在原地振动,不离开位置。
•
O
液体——粒子能滑动、换位置,但仍挨在一起。
•
LID — particles only vibrate about a fixed point; they do not leave their place.
气体——粒子快速、随机、四面八方地飞,相距远。
•
L
•
I
•
Q
•
UID — particles can move around / slide past each other, but stay close together.
•
GAS — particles move fast and randomly in all directions, far apart.
•
When a substance is heated, its particles gain energy and move faster.
加热时,粒子获得能量、动得更快。
•
(Exam: "it gets hotter." Real science: "the particles move faster" — see Exam-vs-Real box.)
(考试说"变热";真科学说"粒子动得更快"——见落差点框。
•
)
•
Solids cannot flow — particles are fixed and only vibrate.
固体不能流——粒子被固定,只振动。
•
Liquids flow and take the shape of the container — particles change places.
液体能流、取容器形状——粒子换位置。
•
Gases flow and fill the container — particles move everywhere.
气体能流、填满容器——粒子到处动。
🔴 RED CARD · 误区红牌
✘
Solid particles do not move at all.
固体粒子完全不动
✔
Solid particles still move — they vibrate in place. They just do not travel from their fixed position.
固体粒子还是动的——在原地振动,只是不离开固定位置。 ---------------------------------------------------------------
TRY · Particle Motion (3 States)
粒子运动（三态）
TRY
Q01Complete5 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
Solid movement: vibrates / moves to and fro / oscillates. Solid forces: very strong. Liquid forces: weaker than solid but stronger than gas. Gas distance: far apart. Gas shape: takes/fills the shape of the container / no fixed shape.
固体运动：振动 / 来回运动。固体粒子间作用力：很强。液体作用力：比固体弱、比气体强。气体粒子距离：相距很远。气体形状：随容器改变 / 没有固定形状。
Accept · 可收：
  • solid particles vibrate
  • solid particles move to and fro
  • solid particles oscillate
  • solid forces are very strong
  • liquid forces are weak compared with solid
  • liquid forces are stronger than gas
  • gas particles are far apart
  • gas fills the container
Q02State / Describe6 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) metal conducts heat better than plastic [accept: plastic is an; insulator]. [1]; (b) particles move faster / vibrate more [accept: gain kinetic energy]. [1]; (c) evaporation [accept: water changes to vapour]. [1].
(a) metal conducts heat better than plastic [accept: plastic is an; insulator]. [1]; (b) particles move faster / vibrate more [accept: gain kinetic energy]. [1]; (c) evaporation [accept: water changes to vapour]. [1].
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) metal conducts heat better than plastic [accept: plastic is an
  • insulator]. [1]
  • (b) particles move faster / vibrate more [accept: gain kinetic energy]. [1]
  • (c) evaporation [accept: water changes to vapour]. [1]
  • (a) metal conducts heat better than plastic [accept: plastic is an; insulator]. [1]; (b) particles move faster / vibrate more [accept: gain kinetic energy]. [1]; (c) evaporation [accept: water changes to vapour]. [1]
  • evaporation
  • evaporate the water
  • heat it to remove water
TEST · Particle Motion (3 States)
粒子运动（三态）
EXAM MODE · NO HINTS · 考试模式
Q03Tick / Underline / State8 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) tick "they move more quickly". [1]; (b)(i) gas. [1]; (b)(ii) liquid. [1]; (b)(iii) condensation / condensing. [1].
(a) tick "they move more quickly". [1]; (b)(i) gas. [1]; (b)(ii) liquid. [1]; (b)(iii) condensation / condensing. [1].
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) tick "they move more quickly". [1]
  • (b)(i) gas. [1]
  • (b)(ii) liquid. [1]
  • (b)(iii) condensation / condensing. [1]
  • (a) tick "they move more quickly". [1]; (b)(i) gas. [1]; (b)(ii) liquid. [1]; (b)(iii) condensation / condensing. [1]
  • (a) tick "they move more quickly". [1].
  • (b)(i) 气体. [1]
Q04Complete / State6 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) solid close / regular / vibrate; liquid close / move slowly / flow.
(a) solid close / regular / vibrate; liquid close / move slowly / flow.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) solid close / regular / vibrate
  • liquid close / move slowly / flow
  • (a) solid close / regular / vibrate; liquid close / move slowly / flow
  • (a) 固体 close / regular / 振动。
  • 液体 close / move slowly / 流动。
  • (a) solid close / regular / vibrate; liquid close / move slowly / flow.
Q05Explain6 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
The fastest-moving particles escape from the surface (evaporate).
The fastest-moving particles escape from the surface (evaporate).
Accept · 可收：
  • The fastest-moving particles escape from the surface (evaporate)
  • the particles left behind are moving more slowly on average / have
  • lower average energy, so the temperature falls. [2]
  • (1 mark: fastest particles leave/escape
  • 1 mark: those left move
  • slower on average → cooler.)
  • The fastest-moving particles escape from the surface (evaporate).
Q06Identify2 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
C (gas).
C (气体)。
Accept · 可收：
  • C (gas)
  • C
  • option C
  • gas
  • C - gas
  • C: gas
  • C (气体)。
Q07State2 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
they vibrate (in place) / vibrate about a fixed.
they vibrate (in place) / vibrate about a fixed.
Accept · 可收：
  • they vibrate (in place) / vibrate about a fixed
  • position
  • they vibrate (in place) / vibrate about a fixed.
  • position.
Q08Explain4 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
Liquid particles can move or slide past each other, so a liquid can flow. Solid particles only vibrate in fixed places and cannot move past each other, so a solid keeps its shape.
液体粒子能互相移动或滑过，所以液体能流动。固体粒子只在固定位置振动，不能互相滑过，所以固体保持形状。
Accept · 可收：
  • liquid particles slide past each other
  • liquid particles can move around each other
  • liquid particles are not fixed in one place
  • solid particles only vibrate
  • solid particles stay in fixed positions
  • solid particles cannot move past each other
  • 液体粒子能互相滑过
Q09State2 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
gas particles move quickly in all directions /.
气体 粒子 移动快 in all directions /。
Accept · 可收：
  • gas particles move quickly in all directions /
  • move randomly and spread out to fill the space
  • 气体 粒子 移动快 in all directions /。
  • move randomly and spread out to fill the space.
Q10Explain6 marks
Expected answer · 标准答案
Heating makes the air particles move faster.
加热 makes the air 粒子 移动更快。
Accept · 可收：
  • Heating makes the air particles move faster
  • faster particles hit
  • the balloon walls more often and harder, so they push out more on
  • the walls. [2]
  • (1 mark particles move faster when heated
  • 1 mark they hit the walls
  • more often/harder → push out more.)
WRAP · Particle Motion (3 States)
粒子运动（三态）
WRAP
📝 SUMMARY · 总结
•
Solid particles vibrate in place; liquid particles slide; gas particles move fast and randomly.
固体粒子原地振动；液体粒子互相滑动；气体粒子快速乱动。
•
Heating makes particles move faster.
加热会让粒子动得更快。
•
Particle motion helps explain flow, diffusion and evaporation.
粒子运动可以解释流动、扩散和蒸发。
🔁 COVER & RECALL · 盖住回想
Heating makes the air particles move faster.
加热 makes the air 粒子 移动更快。

嗨!准备好了吗?
